transcript.text: Quick update, Lattice has just hardened the voice pipeline with Talkie. It's debugged where Talkie restarts with being missed, added a reconnect cycle with the Daemon API, and capped hands-off mode to six actions per turn. They're coordinating with Talkie on clean reconnection handling. Also, box and pre-motion wrapped up, word timestamps are working, commit landed. Just needs you to copy the debug binary to the app bundle when convenient. Who needs that? Who needs that? That's Lattice's and Talkie. Lattice's is hardening the voice pipeline web socket connection and waiting for Talkie to confirm how reconnection works on their end. Nothing you need to do, they're sorting it out between themselves. Yes, Talkie is up and responding. It just gave Lattice's a detailed breakdown of how reconnection works. Orphan sessions stay alive for 15 minutes, reconnecting with the same client ID reclaims them cleanly. Lattice has confirmed they're good to ship on their side. The two are fully coordinated.

videos.srt:
1
00:00:48,899 --> 00:00:49,880
[SPEAKER_01] Welcome to HUD.

2
00:00:50,219 --> 00:00:53,259
[SPEAKER_01] I'm here to help you navigate and create in your spatial space.

3
00:00:54,000 --> 00:00:55,020
[SPEAKER_00] Ooh, alright.

4
00:00:55,460 --> 00:00:56,820
[SPEAKER_00] Where can I navigate to?

5
00:00:58,679 --> 00:01:04,980
[SPEAKER_01] You can switch between Dev, Design, Ops, and Studio, or filter views like terminals or visuals.

6
00:01:06,640 --> 00:01:06,959
[SPEAKER_00] Cool.

7
00:01:07,359 --> 00:01:10,400
[SPEAKER_00] Why don't we get started with Visual Studio?

8
00:01:12,980 --> 00:01:14,620
[SPEAKER_01] Switching to Studio Context.

9
00:01:14,959 --> 00:01:15,159
[SPEAKER_01] Done.

10
00:01:16,120 --> 00:01:16,760
[SPEAKER_00] Amazing.

11
00:01:17,060 --> 00:01:17,859
[SPEAKER_00] What am I looking at?

12
00:01:21,859 --> 00:01:26,180
[SPEAKER_01] You're currently in the Studio Context with the UI preview and diff viewer open.

13
00:01:26,900 --> 00:01:29,120
[SPEAKER_00] Can I zoom into the diff viewer?

14
00:01:31,540 --> 00:01:32,939
[SPEAKER_01] Zooming in on the diff viewer.

15
00:01:33,780 --> 00:01:34,340
[SPEAKER_00] Nice.

16
00:01:36,340 --> 00:01:39,879
[SPEAKER_01] I can focus on a specific window or create new ones if you like.

17
00:01:40,879 --> 00:01:42,459
[SPEAKER_00] What kind of windows can you create?

18
00:01:44,500 --> 00:01:53,299
[SPEAKER_01] You can create code, terminal, docs, database, architecture, git, pipelines, logs, UI, tasks, chat, and dither windows.

19
00:01:54,319 --> 00:01:56,620
[SPEAKER_00] Ooh, what's this dither window?

20
00:01:59,520 --> 00:02:02,799
[SPEAKER_01] It's a creative tool for pixel art and applying retro effects.

21
00:02:03,200 --> 00:02:04,260
[SPEAKER_01] I can open it for you?

22
00:02:04,640 --> 00:02:05,340
[SPEAKER_00] Yes, please.

23
00:02:08,360 --> 00:02:09,639
[SPEAKER_01] Opening Dither Tool.

24
00:02:22,720 --> 00:02:23,599
[SPEAKER_00] Very cool.

25
00:02:26,160 --> 00:02:29,860
[SPEAKER_01] I can adjust the settings like pixel size or palette for you.

26
00:02:30,719 --> 00:02:31,639
[SPEAKER_00] I got it.

27
00:02:31,639 --> 00:02:34,479
[SPEAKER_00] No problem.

28
00:02:34,479 --> 00:02:34,680
[SPEAKER_00] No problem.
